properties.rationale: Blazor WebAssembly SPA hosted by Azure Static Web Apps.
Framework-neutral app services, API clients, localization, and auth state.
Shared wire-contract library referenced by the SPA, app-core, and API.
.NET isolated Azure Functions backend.
Versioned HTTP surface consumed by the SPA. Unversioned aliases remain present in the Functions source.
Battle.net login/callback/logout, current-user session surface, and client-side expired-session detection.
Create, list, inspect, edit, delete, sign up for, and cancel signup for runs.
Current-user guild home and guild settings service.
Current-user profile, character management, enrichment, and cleanup.
Read-only reference lookup service backed by blob-projected reference repositories.
Browser-facing image proxy and cache for Blizzard render-CDN assets served through /api/v1/wow/media/cache.
Health/readiness and admin schema migration endpoints.
Public privacy contact endpoint consumed by the privacy policy page.
Blazor page interactions for browsing run detail and managing signup/cancel actions.
Blazor entry points for Battle.net sign-in, callback failure recovery, sign-out, protected-route redirection, and expired-session redirect.
Client-side resume and HTTP 401 detection probes the current session and redirects expired sessions to login.
Blazor character/profile surface for account characters, selected character, enrichment, and account deletion.
Blazor guild home and current-guild settings pages.
Blazor consumers of static WoW instance, expansion, and specialization reference data.
Blazor utility page that consumes the privacy contact endpoint.
External OAuth service used for login and callback exchange.
External Blizzard profile and guild data API.
External Blizzard static game-data API used by reference-data refresh.
External image URL and byte source consumed through LFM's media cache service.
Free SKU Static Web App hosting the Blazor app.
Function App with system-assigned managed identity.
Dynamic Y1 plan used by the Function App.
.NET 10 isolated worker on Azure Functions v4.
System-assigned identity and RBAC access path for data-plane resources.
Free-tier Cosmos account with local auth disabled.
LFM logical database.
Run documents, partitioned by id.
Raider documents, partitioned by Battle.net id.
Guild documents, partitioned by guild id.
Idempotency replay records for mutating HTTP requests.
Standard_LRS StorageV2 account with shared key access disabled.
Blob container content under reference/{kind}/ for static Blizzard reference data.
Cached Blizzard render image bytes under wow/media-cache/render/{sha256}.{ext} in blob storage.
Durable ASP.NET Data Protection key-ring storage.
Private storage container named deployments, provisioned by the storage module.
RBAC-enabled vault for app secrets and Data Protection key encryption.
Versionless Key Vault key URI used to wrap Data Protection keys.
Workspace-based telemetry component.
Shared workspace for diagnostics and Application Insights.
Metric alert and action group for Cosmos throttling.
Main branch source, infrastructure, and app artifacts after local hook and workflow gates.
Local opt-in hook blocks staged environment files, SSH private keys, certificates, application secrets, Terraform state, and private-key content before commit.
Local opt-in hook runs shell lint, workflow permission checks, locale readiness, .NET format/build, and vulnerable-package audit before push.
Single production environment represented by deploy workflow and environment tags.
Difference closed by the production deploy workflow.
Push or manual deploy event targeting production.
Orchestrator workflow for infra and app deployment.
Deploy workflow job that resolves manual stage input or path-filtered infra/app changes before conditional reusable workflows run.
Reusable Gitleaks workflow that gates deploy app build and infrastructure deployment jobs.
Reusable PSRule for Azure analysis workflow that gates infrastructure deployment.
Reusable workflow that runs Bicep what-if/apply.
Reusable workflow that builds Functions zip and Blazor wwwroot artifacts.
Reusable workflow that uploads the backend and frontend artifacts.
Infrastructure definition delivered by the deploy-infra workflow.
Backend deployable package.
Frontend static bundle.

nodes.label: Player wants to join a run

properties.rationale: Source-inferred entry event for the run-signup flow.
open_question: Confirm actor naming and process boundary with stakeholders.

properties.rationale: Source-inferred process candidate backed by GET /api/v1/runs.
Source-inferred process candidate backed by GET /api/v1/runs/{id}.
Source-inferred process candidate backed by signup options and POST /api/v1/runs/{id}/signup.
Source-inferred alternate path backed by DELETE /api/v1/runs/{id}/signup.
Source-inferred terminal event after a successful signup response.
open_question: Confirm completion event wording with stakeholders.

properties.rationale: Blazor pages and actions for creating, editing, and deleting runs.
Source-backed application process for creating a run document.
Source-backed application process for updating a run document with optimistic concurrency.
Source-backed application process for deleting a run document and its embedded signups.
Blazor account deletion action and post-delete goodbye navigation.
Source-backed application process that deletes the current raider profile and related references.
Site-admin guild management page that reads and updates arbitrary guild documents.
Site-admin guild management service with admin authorization, guild refresh, and settings update behavior.
Admin UI that starts and observes the WoW reference data refresh stream.
Admin and timer-driven reference synchronization service that writes refreshed reference data to blob storage.
Ordered cross-cutting middleware that every HTTP request passes through before a Function handler runs. Registered in api/Program.cs via builder.UseMiddleware in fixed order.
Adds CORS headers and answers preflight OPTIONS; runs first so preflight is not blocked by later auth stages.
Adds standard security response headers, the AGPL section 13 source-code notice, and a default Cache-Control: private, no-store.
Rejects requests whose Content-Length exceeds the configured maximum with 413 Payload Too Large + RFC 9457 problem+json.
Per-IP sliding-window rate limiting across auth, write, and read endpoint tiers; OPTIONS bypasses (handled by CORS).
Emits structured audit records for incoming requests ahead of authentication and authorization.
Resolves the session principal from the request before authorization policy is applied.
Enforces per-route authorization policy on the resolved principal; rejects unauthorized requests before the handler runs.
Durable replay for retried mutations carrying an Idempotency-Key header for an authenticated principal; persists outcomes in the idempotency container.
Shared sliding-window limiter (80/s) wired as a DelegatingHandler on every Blizzard HttpClient; on an upstream 429 it pauses the shared limiter so concurrent callers back off together.

groups.label: Profile service and account deletion process
